title: Benchmark host configuration (constrained-YAML subset)
description: Schema for config/benchmark-hosts/*.yaml. The files use a deliberately constrained YAML subset — flat `key: value` pairs plus one-level sections with two-space-indented `key: value` entries — so the measurement host can parse them without a YAML library. This JSON Schema describes the logical structure after parsing; scripts/test-benchmark-batch.js validates the shipped template against it.

cpu_sets.description: Named CPU sets. Values are comma-separated logical CPU ids with no spaces, chosen from live `lscpu -e=CPU,CORE,SOCKET,NODE,ONLINE` output: distinct physical cores, no SMT siblings, same socket/NUMA node by default. Empty string = not yet filled (batch refuses to run).

labs.description: Lab id → cpu_set name. Every enabled lab must be mapped; the referenced set must exist and satisfy the lab's LAB_CPU_SET_REQUIREMENT / LAB_MIN_CPUS.
